This plant has been classified within the genus Humboldtia, which is part of the larger family Sapindaceae. intro: Humboldtia elegans, also known as the "Madhuvasa" or "Fragrant Yellow Flower", is a flowering plant species native to the Western Ghats region of India. Traditionally, Humboldtia elegans has been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant's bioactive constituents include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Humboldtia elegans include teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as topical creams and ointments used to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
